data.title: One Heung Kong in Guangzhou earns three international certifications for sustainability and architecture.
data.description: In Guangzhou, China, on January 21, 2026, One Heung Kong, a top-tier luxury residence in the Greater Bay Area, has reached a significant accomplishment in sustainable architecture. The property has successfully obtained certification from the World Green Design Organization (WGDO) with the prestigious LEED Platinum rating. This achievement showcases the commitment of One Heung Kong towards environmentally-friendly practices and designing buildings that adhere to green standards.

data.title: AAVE priced at $158.35; Predicted $190-195 by Feb 2026 despite current RSI of 43.80 and bearish MACD signal.
data.description: The current trading price for AAVE is $158.35, with market analysts predicting a potential increase to the range of $190-$195 by February 2026. However, the Relative Strength Index (RSI) is currently at 43.80, and a bearish Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) signal indicates a need for caution due to approaching critical support levels.

data.title: Openmarkets to merge with Lake Superior Acq Corp in agreement. Trading tech provider joins with SPAC.
data.description: On January 23, 2026, technology provider Openmarkets Group Pty Ltd ("Openmarkets") based in Sydney and New York announced a merger and business combination agreement with Lake Superior Acquisition Corp. ("Lake Superior"). This agreement marks a significant step in the company's strategy for growth and expansion. Openmarkets, specializing in Trading and Wealth Management, will be merging with Lake Superior in a deal that will see substantial investment and new opportunities. The agreement is set to bring a significant influx of capital, expected to be in the range of [specific amount], enhancing the company's position in the market. Openmarkets aims to leverage this partnership to further develop and offer innovative solutions in the trading and wealth management sector, solidifying its presence as a key player in the industry.

data.title: CEO of Capital One warns Trump's 10% interest rate cap on credit cards could lead to recession, impacting $6 trillion consumer spending.
data.description: Capital One Financial Corp. CEO Richard Fairbank expressed concerns over President Trump's proposed 10% cap on credit card interest rates, warning of potential consequences during the company's fourth-quarter earnings call. Fairbank stated that such a move could restrict consumer access to credit and destabilize the economy. He emphasized that implementing price controls would not make credit more affordable but rather limit its availability for consumers across different credit profiles. Fairbank highlighted the significant role of consumer credit in the U.S. economy, noting that 70% of GDP is driven by consumer spending, with $6 trillion attributed to such expenditure. He indicated that banks would likely have to slash credit lines, restrict accounts, and limit new originations if the proposal were enforced, potentially leading to a recession.
